'''Borborygmus Gog''' was the [[Shi'ido]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who was placed in charge of [[Project Starscream]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].

==Biography==
During his early [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Gog attended the [[Chandrilan Academy of Sciences]] along with [[Mammon Hoole]]. The two were later hired by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to counter the [[Bioweapon/Legends|bioweapons]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

In their labs on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kiva]], where they were moved after the so-called [[Showdown on Coruscant|Jedi Rebellion]], Gog and Hoole tinkered with the ability to control life and [[Death/Legends|death]]. Hoole became alarmed when he realized their experiments were draining the life from Kiva. Despite this, Gog decided to continue the experiments. The experiments eventually wiped out all life on the planet, including the [[Kivan]]s. The experiments on Kiva were deemed a failure, which Gog blamed on Hoole. Gog subsequently continued Project Starscream in secret. Unfortunately for Gog, his former partner always seemed to show up to put an end to his experiments. Gog researched [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and created the "[[Essence Stealer]]," which would rip out life essences, and store them in a crystal matrix. The essences could then be studied. Gog hoped to remove the life-essence of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] by posting legends of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] under the name of [[Borborygmus Gog|ForceFlow]] to attract [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] individuals to the space station, [[Nespis VIII]]. One such individual was [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash Arranda]], the niece of Mammon Hoole.

Gog was believed to have fallen to his death on Nespis VIII, but reappeared on Kiva hoping to capture Hoole and regain control over Project Starscream. Gog activated the biological weapon [[Eppon]], but it turned on him, forcing him to kill it. Gog himself was executed by the vengeful wraiths of the dead Kivans, after it was revealed that he was the one responsible for their deaths.

==Behind the scenes==
The character's name is spelled '''Borborygamous Gog''' on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]. This is probably a typographical error.
